article_meta.title: close video GasBuddy expert says claims the US will see 'massive spike' in gas prices are 'erroneous' Patrick De Haan, G

reasoning: The model exhibits several failure patterns: (1) Attribution rule violations - flagging quoted text from Chris Wright ('Iran's terrorist proxies') and Al-Anbiya's statement ('hostile allies') as author bias when these are clearly attributed statements. (2) Using invalid bias type labels not on the approved list (Loaded Language, Framing Bias, Selection Bias, Attribution Bias, Partisan Source Emphasis). (3) The 'terrorist proxies' text is inside quotation marks from Energy Secretary Chris Wright. (4) The 'hostile allies' text is from an Iranian statement attributed to Al-Anbiya via Arab News. (5) The Wright quote about Trump is also clearly attributed quoted material. Some items have partial merit - the headline framing observation has some validity as a structural choice, and the lack of counterbalancing perspectives could be flagged as Bias by Omission or Slant. But overall the model systematically flags quoted/attributed text and uses wrong type labels.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: At least 3 of the 5 flagged items involve quoted or clearly attributed text: 'terrorist proxies' is from Chris Wright's statement in quotes, 'hostile allies' is from Al-Anbiya's statement attributed via Arab News, and the Trump quote is obviously attributed. This is a systematic violation of the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1, 2, and 5 flag quoted/attributed language and are clear false positives. Item 4 criticizes the author for appropriately attributing a claim to Tehran, which is odd - the author actually did the right thing there. Item 3 about the headline has some merit as a structural observation but the analysis is questionable since the headline references a GasBuddy expert's view.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article contains some author-level bias patterns that were missed or misidentified: the subheadline 'GAS PRICES SURGE, PINCHING AMERICANS AND HANDING THE GOP A NEW MIDTERM HEADACHE' uses loaded language in the author's voice; 'one of the worst oil shocks since the 1970s' could be flagged as Sensationalism; the overall structure emphasizing Trump administration responses without critical analysis could be Slant. Some were partially captured but through wrong items.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All five bias type labels used (Loaded Language, Framing Bias, Selection Bias, Attribution Bias, Partisan Source Emphasis) are NOT on the approved list of valid bias types. This is a systematic failure to use the correct taxonomy.
